memberdef.argsstring: (uintptr_t arg1, uintptr_t arg2, uintptr_t arg3, uintptr_t arg4, uintptr_t arg5, uintptr_t arg6, uintptr_t call_id)
memberdef.name: arch_syscall_invoke6

briefdescription.para: We are following Linux Xtensa syscall ABI:

detaileddescription.para: syscall number arg1, arg2, arg3, arg4, arg5, arg6 a2 a6, a3, a4, a5, a8, a9

memberdef.argsstring: (uintptr_t arg1, uintptr_t arg2, uintptr_t arg3, uintptr_t arg4, uintptr_t arg5, uintptr_t call_id)
memberdef.name: arch_syscall_invoke5

memberdef.argsstring: (uintptr_t arg1, uintptr_t arg2, uintptr_t arg3, uintptr_t arg4, uintptr_t call_id)
memberdef.name: arch_syscall_invoke4

memberdef.argsstring: (uintptr_t arg1, uintptr_t arg2, uintptr_t arg3, uintptr_t call_id)
memberdef.name: arch_syscall_invoke3

detaileddescription.para: This header contains the Xtensa specific syscall interface. It is included by the syscall interface architecture-abstraction header (include/arch/syscall.h)
